metadata.title: Competing infrastructure corridors — BRI vs IMEC vs PGII/Lobito vs Global Gateway vs INSTC vs Middle Corridor (overlaps & inconsistencies, dated)
metadata.status: the corridors, their sponsors, launch dates, and overlapping memberships are FACT (G7/G20/EU/national announcements, press of record); 'trend conclusions' are stated as DOCUMENTED overlapping participation, not attributed motives ('X feels Y' avoided per the composition/division-fallacy guard)
metadata.frame: Maps the rival trade/transport-corridor programs and — the user's key ask — their INCONSISTENCIES: the same states sponsor multiple, often rival, corridors. China's BRI (2013) vs the US/India/Gulf/EU IMEC (2023) vs the G7's PGII + Lobito Corridor vs the EU's Global Gateway vs the Russia-Iran-India INSTC vs the Russia-bypassing Middle Corridor. The pattern (stated as documented participation, not imputed motive): India sits in BOTH IMEC (US/Gulf/EU) AND INSTC (Russia/Iran); the EU backs IMEC, Global Gateway, Lobito AND the Middle Corridor; the Gulf states (in BRICS) co-sponsor the US-led IMEC; China anchors both BRI and the Middle Corridor. Attaches to China/India/Russia/Iran/EU/US_Government/Gulf + BRICS. Anchor for #144 (alignment matrix), #145 (String of Pearls), #146 (OPEC).

the_corridors: (1) BRI — China's Belt and Road Initiative (2013), the original: land 'belt' + maritime 'road' across ~150 countries; debt-sustainability critiques are real but the blanket 'debt-trap' framing is itself contested (Rhodium/Chatham House found it overstated). (2) IMEC — India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (G20, Sept 2023): India->UAE/Saudi->Israel/Jordan->EU rail+ports+H2+data; STALLED by the post-Oct-2023 Gaza war (the Israel link). (3) PGII — the G7's Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment (2022), with the LOBITO CORRIDOR (Angola-DRC-Zambia rail to the Atlantic) for copper/cobalt, US+EU-backed. (4) Global Gateway — the EU's EUR300B answer to BRI (2021). (5) INSTC — the International North-South Transport Corridor (Russia-Iran-India via the Caspian), sanctions-resilient. (6) Middle Corridor — the Trans-Caspian route (China-Central Asia-Caucasus-Turkey-EU) that BYPASSES Russia. grade: fact.
the_inconsistencies: Documented overlapping/rival memberships (the trend, stated as participation not motive): INDIA participates in IMEC (with the US, Gulf, EU, Israel) AND in INSTC (with Russia and Iran) AND in BRICS — while declining BRI. The EU backs IMEC, Global Gateway, the Lobito Corridor AND the Middle Corridor. SAUDI ARABIA and the UAE co-sponsor the US-led IMEC while being BRICS members and BRI participants. TURKEY anchors the Middle Corridor (which routes around Russia) while remaining a major buyer of Russian energy. CHINA anchors BRI and is the eastern origin of the Middle Corridor that Western backers promote to bypass Russia. grade: fact (these are documented memberships; no unitary 'intent' imputed to any bloc).
the_reading_discipline: Per the composition/division-fallacy guard: a state's simultaneous membership in rival corridors is recorded as INSTITUTIONAL PARTICIPATION, not as a feeling, a plan, or a unitary national 'mind.' The defensible trend-conclusion is structural: corridor membership is non-exclusive and hedged — multi-alignment is the norm, not the exception — which makes 'bloc' framings (West vs BRICS) leaky descriptions of who actually builds what with whom. grade: trend stated structurally (overlay).
the_honest_reading: There is no single 'China vs the West' corridor map; there is a lattice of overlapping, partly-rival programs in which the same states appear on multiple sides. The dated facts — BRI (2013), Global Gateway (2021), PGII/Lobito (2022), IMEC (2023, since stalled by Gaza), INSTC and the Middle Corridor as Russia-related hedges — show corridor membership is non-exclusive. The corpus records the overlaps as participation (not motive), draws the structural trend (multi-alignment/hedging is the norm), and leaves intent unattributed. Anchor for the alignment-matrix (#144), String of Pearls (#145), and OPEC (#146) blocks. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
